page: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 shared vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores using the x86_64 architecture. The underlying hardware is powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. This configuration does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng falls into the bottom 10%, and the Geekbench single-core score is in the middle 50%. However, multi-core and parallelized execution show stronger results. The Passmark CPU score, extended instructions test, encryption test, and physics test all rank in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench ray tracing also achieve top 25% performance. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth are in the top 25%, while uncached large-block read bandwidth and the overall Passmark memory score remain in the middle 50%.

For database and application workloads, Redis SET operations perform in the top 25%, while Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput fall in the middle 50%. Large-language model inference speeds are model-dependent; prompt processing for a small 135M model is in the top 25%, but text generation for a medium 7B model drops to the bottom 10%. Given the shared CPU allocation and the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling. It is suitable for large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, multi-threaded data processing, and memory-heavy general-purpose applications.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 64 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 512.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory density of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core execution, Passmark CPU tests, and Redis SET operations perform in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is strong for cached reads and large-block writes. Given its shared CPU allocation and massive memory capacity, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 KVM virtual server with 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering strong multi-core and memory write performance.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a KVM virtual server with 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixty-four shared cores and half a terabyte of RAM for memory-intensive parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU, encryption, and physics benchmark performance
High-throughput memory bandwidth for cached reads and large-block writes
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU resources
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded processing
